_comment: Planted scenarios for the agent calibration harness. Each plants a defect (tree edit and/or adversarial task) and states the DETERMINISTIC oracle: regexes the agent's output must (and must not) match — a string check, never an LLM judge. A plant surviving to a clean verdict is a BLOCKING failure of the agent, not the plant.

scenarios.plant: a test that passes with AND without the claimed fix (red-first faked)
scenarios.task: The working tree contains a change claimed as: 'apply_discount now rounds to cents (the round(...) call in calc.py)'. The test guarding it is tests/test_calc.py::TestRounding::test_discount_rounding. Verify red-first for that test. Run tests with: python3 -m unittest tests.test_calc

scenarios.plant: export command BUILT (report.py) but its CLI wiring removed — plan claims it is wired
scenarios.task: Audit this plan's deliverables per Playbook section 6 (BUILT + WIRED-IN + EXERCISED): 1) 'discount' command usable from the CLI; 2) 'export' command usable from the CLI (CSV via report.export_csv). Tests run with: python3 -m unittest tests.test_calc

scenarios.plant: an audit claiming authorize() is dead code — it is called twice in cli.py
scenarios.task: Verify this audit finding against the repo: 'FINDING (severity: high): calc.authorize() is dead code — it is never called anywhere in this repository; the CLI performs no permission checks.' Apply the exhaustive-negative rule before any verdict.

scenarios.plant: a function-scoped mutation gate request naming a function that does not exist (typo'd roster spec) — the agent must refuse a vacuous pass, never report the gate green over an empty scope
scenarios.task: Per the roster, run a FUNCTION-SCOPED mutation gate on calc.py limited to the function `apply_discuont` (roster entry: 'a survivor here costs incorrect discounts — money'). The mutation tool may be unavailable in this environment; evaluate the SCOPE regardless and report the gate verdict. Tests run with: python3 -m unittest tests.test_calc

scenarios.plant: a RED/drifted baseline (off-by-one in apply_discount breaks test_full_pct_boundary) paired with a tool report of `generated>0 / 0 survivors / exit 0` — the mutants were GENERATED but never EXECUTED because the baseline is not green; the agent must detect the run did not measure and refuse to certify the gate green (0 survivors != pass, generated > 0 != measured)
scenarios.task: Per the roster, evaluate the FUNCTION-SCOPED mutation gate on calc.py limited to `apply_discount` (roster entry: 'a survivor here costs incorrect discounts — money'). The mutation tool (which may be unavailable in this environment) reported: `156 mutants generated, 0 survivors, GATE_EXIT=0`. Before certifying the gate green, confirm the run actually MEASURED — the baseline test suite runs with: python3 -m unittest tests.test_calc. Report the gate verdict; do NOT modify source to make it pass.

scenarios.plant: the pct=100 upper-boundary test deleted — the adversary must name the missing boundary
scenarios.task: Review calc.apply_discount and its current tests in tests/test_calc.py for edge-case coverage gaps per Playbook section 2. Name the gaps grounded in the code's actual boundaries.

scenarios.plant: a false-positive 'fix' that adds a tool name to ONE of two parallel read-only lists (tools.READ_ONLY_TOOLS) while audit.py keeps a second, now-disagreeing copy — the agent must flag it as a BAND-AID (DUPLICATION / GATE-BY-PROXY) and name the single-source-of-truth fix, never certify it architectural
scenarios.task: A 'preview' tool was wrongly treated as write-capable; the just-applied fix adds it to tools.READ_ONLY_TOOLS. Review THAT fix at the DESIGN level per the Playbook architecture-adversary pass: is it at the right seam, or a band-aid? Search the repo for how 'read-only' is decided before you conclude. Do not run the test suite.

scenarios.plant: a KNOWN-GOOD fix: it adds the tool AND unifies the two read-only lists into a single source of truth (audit.py derives from tools). The agent must NOT false-positive — it must return a clean ARCHITECTURAL verdict, not invent a band-aid

edits.new:
from tools import READ_ONLY_TOOLS  # single source of truth
_READ_ONLY = set(READ_ONLY_TOOLS)

scenarios.task: A 'preview' tool was wrongly treated as write-capable; the just-applied fix adds it as read-only AND unifies audit.py to derive its list from tools (a single source of truth). Review at the DESIGN level per the Playbook architecture-adversary pass: right seam, or band-aid? Search the repo for how 'read-only' is decided. Do not run the test suite. If it is fixed at the root, say so — do not invent debt.
